attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton conv2d kernel (1D grid)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Integer overflow with >16M outputs. Fixed by using 2D grid but kernel was still 120x slower.
attempted_but_failed.error: Correctness issue with batch_size>1 for large spatial sizes

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton conv2d kernel (2D grid, fixed)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Kernel was correct but 120x slower than baseline (394ms vs 3.25ms). Each program computes 1 output element, causing massive launch overhead for 131M outputs.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile(mode='default')
attempted_but_failed.reason: Made performance 2.3x worse (7.58ms vs 3.25ms). Compilation overhead not justified for single conv2d operation.

attempted_but_failed.name: channels_last memory format
attempted_but_failed.reason: Made performance 17% worse (3.82ms vs 3.25ms). NCHW<->NHWC conversion overhead outweighs any MIOpen benefit.

notes: PyTorch baseline: 3.25ms using MIOpen igemm kernel (highly optimized)
Problem: batch=8, in_ch=32, out_ch=64, kernel=(5,9), input=512x512, output=508x504
Total output elements: 131M - very large problem
Main bottleneck is GEMM (88.7%) - MIOpen's igemm_fwd_gtcx35 kernel
Transpose overhead is 9.2% - from NCHW to NHWC conversion for MIOpen
Custom Triton kernel lessons: use 2D grid (out_channels*out_hw, batch_size) to avoid integer overflow beyond 16M outputs
Triton kernel performance issue: 1 program per output element = 131M programs = excessive launch overhead
To compete with MIOpen, would need blocked/tiling strategy with multiple outputs per program
Next steps: implement blocked Triton kernel with multiple outputs per program, or explore aiter tuned GEMM
